draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners works better with an encouraging, conversational tone rather than neutral/technical reporting
facade.label: Motivational coach voice
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers personality-driven communication; likely wants encouragement, casual language, emoji/exclamation marks, personal touch
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity and restraint; likely wants factual, no-nonsense updates, minimal personality, professional tone

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer soft, understated UI with subtle light accents over high-contrast or flat design
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + organic vs digital
facade.label: Glowing minimal
facade.acceptImplies: Soft glow, rounded corners, gentle gradients, night-friendly interface that feels human-scaled and calm
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer either sharper aesthetics, bolder contrast, or more playful/energetic visual language

evidence.hypothesis: A weather app for runners works better with an encouraging, conversational tone rather than neutral/technical reporting
evidence.implication: User prefers personality-driven communication; likely wants encouragement, casual language, emoji/exclamation marks, personal touch

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer clean, high-contrast layouts that are easy to scan at a glance during outdoor activity
facade.label: Bright minimalist cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values clarity and simplicity over visual richness; likely prefers sans-serif fonts, ample whitespace, and high contrast for outdoor legibility
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer either more visual density/richness, or softer/muted color palettes; readability in bright sunlight might not be the primary concern

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners need persistent access to key metrics (pace, distance, time) without interrupting map view
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation and information hierarchy — persistent sidebar vs modal overlays vs integrated HUD
facade.label: Sidebar with running stats
facade.acceptImplies: User values constant metric visibility and side-panel navigation; layout should anchor stats to left edge, keep map/route prominent in main area
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers stats hidden until needed, or integrated into top/bottom bars; sidebar feels like wasted space on small screens or too cluttered

facade.hypothesis: Runners want either granular splits (pace per km, heart rate zones) or one clean number (overall effort)—not both mixed on screen
facade.axisTargeted: Information density: detailed metrics vs single key stat
facade.label: Splits vs summary
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers seeing detailed breakdowns of their run (pace per segment, cadence trends, elevation). Design should show data-rich views with tables or scrollable metric cards.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers glanceable single metrics or rolled-up summaries (total time, avg pace, estimated effort). Design should hide complexity behind card hierarchies or progress rings.

facade.hypothesis: Runners expect motion timing that mirrors their physical rhythm, not arbitrary app transitions
facade.axisTargeted: fluid vs snappy — does motion rhythm follow human biology or device interaction patterns
facade.label: Pace synced to breathing
facade.acceptImplies: Animations should breathe with the user (slower on rest screens, faster during active running). Transitions feel biological, not mechanical. This runner app needs a pulse.
facade.rejectImplies: Motion should be device-driven and instant—no artificial pacing. Animations serve function only, not physiological mirroring. Speed is neutral, not expressive.

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners prioritizes clean, factual information delivery over personality-driven messaging—users want speed and accuracy over encouragement
facade.label: No-nonsense data
facade.acceptImplies: User values efficiency and clarity; prefers technical precision, minimal emoji, straightforward language, facts-first layout. Personality is secondary to utility.
facade.rejectImplies: User confirms preference for personality-driven approach; dismisses purely functional/neutral tone as cold or demotivating for their running context.

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind, humidity, and pace-friendly forecasts to help runners plan their routes and timing.
draft.html:
RunWeather

San Francisco, CA

62°F
Partly Cloudy
Feels like 59°F
Wind
8 mph
Humidity
65%
UV Index
4
Visibility
10 mi
Hourly Forecast
Now 62°F Partly Cloudy
2:00 PM 64°F Sunny
4:00 PM 61°F Cloudy
6:00 PM 58°F Cloudy
